# UC-15.1.29 · APC Network Management Card SNMP Communication Loss

## Description

`upsCommStatus` enumerates whether the UPS management agent can see the hardware. Value `2` (`commLost`) means SNMP dashboards go blind while the UPS may still be passing power—exactly when you need remote visibility before an outage.

## Value

Cuts silent monitoring gaps on APC NMCs by alerting when polling sees communication lost while the device remains in service.

## Implementation

Confirm enum mapping for your firmware (`1=ok`, `2=commLost` per classic PowerNet). Some deployments emit string extractions—normalize with `case()`. If you only receive traps, search `snmp:trap` for APC enterprise OID `.1.3.6.1.4.1.318` and `communicationLost`. Combine with host ping to separate network cuts from dead cards.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• `upsCommStatus` polled at least every 5 minutes.
• NetOps escalation for VLAN ACL mistakes.

Step 1 — Validate enum with `snmpget`.

Step 2 — Correlate with switch port events.

Step 3 — Spare NMC RMA process.

Step 4 — Redundant polling host to avoid false commLost from collector downtime.

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval comm=tonumber(upsCommStatus)
| stats latest(comm) as upsCommStatus by host
| where upsCommStatus=2
```

## Visualization

Table (hosts with commLost), Timeline (recovery), Single value (affected count).
